items.title: How vibe-coding app Anything is rebuilding after getting booted from the App Store twice
items.content: Anything is planning to launch a desktop companion app to aid mobile app development after its App Store removal.

items.title: Amazon to buy Globalstar for $11.57B in bid to flesh out its satellite biz
items.content: Amazon on Tuesday agreed to buy satellite company Globalstar, known for powering Apple's "Emergency SOS" feature, for $11.57 billion in cash.

items.title: Apple reportedly testing four designs for upcoming smart glasses
items.content: These glasses are a step back from an ambitious plan that once called for Apple to launch a variety of mixed and augmented reality devices.

items.title: Apple’s foldable iPhone is on track to launch in September, report says
items.content: The news follows a report from Nikkei Asia on Tuesday that raised concerns the company’s foldable iPhone could be delayed due to challenges during the phone’s engineering test

items.title: Microsoft is officially killing its Outlook Lite app next month
items.content: Launched in 2022, Outlook Lite is a lightweight version of the regular Outlook app, designed for Android phones with limited storage and regions with slower internet connectio

items.title: Microsoft is working on yet another OpenClaw-like agent
items.content: The new features would be geared toward enterprise customers, with better security controls than the famously risky open source OpenClaw agent.

items.title: WireGuard VPN developer can’t ship software updates after Microsoft locks account
items.content: The popular open source VPN maker is the second high-profile developer to say Microsoft locked his account without notifying him and is blocking their ability to send software

items.title: Developer of VeraCrypt encryption software says Windows users may face boot-up issues after Microsoft locked his account
items.content: The maker of the popular open source file encryption software VeraCrypt said Microsoft locked his online account, which may prevent device owners from booting up their compute

items.title: Trump Threatens to Fire Powell if He Does Not Resign From Fed
items.content: Although Jerome H. Powell’s term as Fed chair officially ends on May 15, he can stay on as a governor until 2028 and said he would not leave until a criminal investigation was

items.title: Trump Threatens to Fire Powell, Again
items.content: The new threat against the Fed chair comes ahead of a Senate confirmation hearing for Kevin Warsh, President Trump’s pick to lead the central bank.

items.title: Trump Increased Tax Refunds, but a Political Challenge Still Looms
items.content: While last year’s tax law has raised the average I.R.S. refund, the boost is less than the White House expected — and may not have much impact on voters.

items.title: Japan Pledges $10 Billion to Help Countries Cope With Oil Prices
items.content: With supply chains at risk, the aid to Southeast Asian nations is aimed at securing the availability of oil-based products, including medical equipment.
